message:
fix(planner): include feature flags in plan cache key, bypass cache for cracking hints

The LRU query plan cache keyed plans solely on (sql_template, schema_cookie),
which meant two executions of the same SQL with different PlannerFeatureFlags
(e.g. leapfrog_join=true vs false) would incorrectly alias to the same
cached plan. This caused the second execution to silently use the wrong
join strategy.

This commit fixes both cache-correctness issues:

1. plan_cache_key_with_feature_flags() appends the leapfrog_join and
   dpccp_join booleans to the xxh3 hash material, so different feature-flag
   combinations produce distinct cache entries.

2. When adaptive cracking hints are provided, the cache is bypassed entirely
   (early return before cache lookup). Cracking hints are mutable runtime
   state derived from data distribution sampling — they can legitimately
   change the preferred index for the same SQL template across executions,
   making cached plans invalid.

Test coverage:
- test_query_planner_cache_separates_feature_flag_variants: verifies that
  hash-only and leapfrog plans for the same SQL produce different Arc
  pointers and the cache holds both entries
- test_query_planner_cache_bypasses_adaptive_cracking_hints: verifies that
  passing a CrackingHintStore causes a fresh plan computation even when a
  cached plan exists for the same template

Updated E2E artifact: planner improvements reduced full_table_scan count
from 39→27 and selection_elapsed_us from 60→50.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
